trigger_phrases: event loop lag instrumentation reindex scan
trigger embedding backfill chunk and yield
event loop lag sampler trigger backfill chunk plan
derived.causal_summary: This phase is the direct follow-on to 020. 018 made the reindex scan cancellable with cooperative yields in its two big tail loops; 019 wrote a maintenance marker so a launcher adopts a busy daemon instead of reaping it; 020 extended that marker to the post-scan embedding queue. 020's own limitation opens this phase: the marker keeps the daemon un-reaped but not responsive. A blocked event loop can neither answer a competing launcher's bridge/probe nor fire the marker's 20s self-refresh, so a synchronous phase that blocks longer than the 180s TTL lets the marker go stale and a second daemon spawns. The exact ~79s blocking phase was never pinned, and static analysis shows that with the trigger-embedding-backfill flag off no enumerated synchronous phase should block that long (the main loop and LIMIT-5 tail phases already yield because embeddings are out-of-process; the orphan sweep is bounded to 200 rows). So this phase instruments the scan with an event-loop lag sampler plus per-phase wall-clock to pin a true block apart from slow-but-cooperative work, chunks the one unbounded synchronous transaction (trigger-backfill's whole-corpus phrase sync) so it yields between chunk transactions and is cancellable, and refreshes the marker on entry to each un-yielded tail phase. The launcher adopt/reap path was read end-to-end and confirmed correct (a fresh marker already causes adoption), so it is unchanged. The live single-launcher lag read is the deploy-time confirmation.
